title: [Bug]: Internal Error: cannot create iterator from type *provider_config.HDFSFileStoreConfig
author: dsalahutdinov
body:
### Expected Behavior

Creating HDFS work fine for client code:

``` python
local_executor = ff.SparkCredentials(
    master="local[*]",  # использовать все доступные CPU ядра
    deploy_mode="client",
    python_version="3.10"
)
creds = ff.BasicCredentials(
    username="username",
    password="password"
)

hdfs = ff.register_hdfs(
    name="local-hdfs",
    host="namenode", 
    port="9000",
    path="/data/featureform",
    description="Local HDFS store for testing",
    team="local-team",
    credentials=creds,
    hdfs_site_file="/featureform/hdfs-site.xml",
    core_site_file="/featureform/core-site.xml"
)

spark = ff.register_spark(
    name="spark",
    description="Local Spark connected to local HDFS",
    team="local-team",
    executor=local_executor,
    filestore=hdfs
)
```

### Actual Behavior


It fails with `Internal Error: cannot create iterator from type *provider_config.HDFSFileStoreConfig`. Here is logs:
```
{
   "level":"error",
   "ts":1763726765.5395706,
   "logger":"global-logger",
   "caller":"interceptors/interceptors.go:27",
   "msg":"GRPCError",
   "error":"Internal Error: cannot create iterator from type *provider_config.HDFSFileStoreConfig\n",
   "method":"/featureform.serving.metadata.proto.Metadata/CreateProvider",
   "request":"provider:{name:\"spark5\" description:\"Local Spark connected to local HDFS\" type:\"SPARK_OFFLINE\" software:\"spark\" team:\"local-team\" serialized_config:\"{\\\"ExecutorType\\\": \\\"SPARK\\\", \\\"StoreType\\\": \\\"HDFS\\\", \\\"ExecutorConfig\\\": {\\\"Master\\\": \\\"local[*]\\\", \\\"DeployMode\\\": \\\"client\\\", \\\"PythonVersion\\\": \\\"3.10.10\\\", \\\"CoreSite\\\": \\\"\\\", \\\"YarnSite\\\": \\\"\\\"}, \\\"StoreConfig\\\": {\\\"Host\\\": \\\"namenode\\\", \\\"Port\\\": \\\"9000\\\", \\\"Path\\\": \\\"/data/featureform\\\", \\\"HDFSSiteConf\\\": \\\"<configuration><property><name>fs.default.name</name><value>local-hdfs</value></property></configuration>\\\", \\\"CoreSiteConf\\\": \\\"<configuration>\\\\n<property><name>fs.defaultFS</name><value>local-hdfs</value></property>\\\\n</configuration>\\\", \\\"CredentialType\\\": \\\"BasicCredential\\\", \\\"CredentialConfig\\\": {\\\"Username\\\": \\\"username\\\", \\\"Password\\\": \\\"password\\\"}}}\" tags:{} properties:{}} request_id:\"b5fd12fe-bb7f-4af8-8650-2504c0f2b8be\"",
   "response":"<nil>",
   "stack_trace":{
      "root":{
         "message":"cannot create iterator from type *provider_config.HDFSFileStoreConfig",
         "stack":[
            "grpc.(*Server).handleStream:/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/grpc@v1.70.0/server.go:1810",
            "grpc.(*Server).processUnaryRPC:/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/grpc@v1.70.0/server.go:1400",
            "proto._Metadata_CreateProvider_Handler:/app/metadata/proto/metadata_grpc.pb.go:811",
            "interceptors.UnaryServerErrorInterceptor:/app/helpers/interceptors/interceptors.go:22",
            "proto._Metadata_CreateProvider_Handler.func1:/app/metadata/proto/metadata_grpc.pb.go:809",
            "metadata.(*MetadataServer).CreateProvider:/app/metadata/metadata.go:3314",
            "metadata.(*MetadataServer).genericCreate:/app/metadata/metadata.go:3597",
            "metadata.(*providerResource).Update:/app/metadata/metadata.go:1868",
            "metadata.(*providerResource).isValidConfigUpdate:/app/metadata/metadata.go:1910",
            "metadata.isValidSparkConfigUpdate:/app/metadata/provider.go:213",
            "provider_config.SparkConfig.DifferingFields:/app/provider/provider_config/spark_config.go:269",
            "provider_config.(*HDFSFileStoreConfig).DifferingFields:/app/provider/provider_config/hdfs_file_store_config.go:165",
            "provider_config.differingFields:/app/provider/provider_config/provider_config.go:28",
            "struct_iterator.NewStructIterator:/app/helpers/struct_iterator/struct_iterator.go:72",
            "fferr.NewInternalError:/app/fferr/misc.go:20",
            "fferr.newBaseError:/app/fferr/errors.go:201",
            "fferr.NewGenericError:/app/fferr/generic.go:23"
         ]
      }
   },
   "stacktrace":"github.com/featureform/helpers/interceptors.UnaryServerErrorInterceptor\n\t/app/helpers/interceptors/interceptors.go:27\ngithub.com/featureform/metadata/proto._Metadata_CreateProvider_Handler\n\t/app/metadata/proto/metadata_grpc.pb.go:811\ngoogle.golang.org/grpc.(*Server).processUnaryRPC\n\t/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/grpc@v1.70.0/server.go:1400\ngoogle.golang.org/grpc.(*Server).handleStream\n\t/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/grpc@v1.70.0/server.go:1810\ngoogle.golang.org/grpc.(*Server).serveStreams.func2.1\n\t/go/pkg/mod/google.golang.org/grpc@v1.70.0/server.go:1030"
}
```

It is probably because of getting `t := reflect.TypeOf(s).Kind()` of pointer type `s *HDFSFileStoreConfig`, [passed to differingFields method](https://github.com/featureform/featureform/blob/main/provider/provider_config/hdfs_file_store_config.go#L165C9-L165C24), [here](https://github.com/featureform/featureform/blob/main/helpers/struct_iterator/struct_iterator.go#L70C2-L70C31) return reflect.Ptr instead of reflect.Struct

### Steps To Reproduce

Run

``` python
local_executor = ff.SparkCredentials(
    master="local[*]",  # использовать все доступные CPU ядра
    deploy_mode="client",
    python_version="3.10"
)
creds = ff.BasicCredentials(
    username="username",
    password="password"
)

hdfs = ff.register_hdfs(
    name="local-hdfs",
    host="namenode", 
    port="9000",
    path="/data/featureform",
    description="Local HDFS store for testing",
    team="local-team",
    credentials=creds,
    hdfs_site_file="/featureform/hdfs-site.xml",
    core_site_file="/featureform/core-site.xml"
)

spark = ff.register_spark(
    name="spark",
    description="Local Spark connected to local HDFS",
    team="local-team",
    executor=local_executor,
    filestore=hdfs
)
```

You will get:
```
((feastenv) ) root@92a7d3073c99:/app# featureform apply --insecure --host featureform:7878 /featureform/user_features.py
DeprecationWarning: ast.Num is deprecated and will be removed in Python 3.14; use ast.Constant instead
Applying Run: 2025-11-21t12-06-05
Creating User default_owner
Creating Provider postgres_offline
Creating Provider redis
Creating Provider local-hdfs
Creating Provider spark5
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/feastenv/bin/featureform", line 7, in <module>
    sys.exit(cli())
             ^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1462, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1383, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1850, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
                           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1246, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/click/core.py", line 814, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/cli.py", line 223, in apply
    client.apply(asynchronous=asynchronous, verbose=verbose)
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/register.py", line 4975, in apply
    resource_state.create_all(
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/resources.py", line 3295, in create_all
    created_variant, existing_variant = resource._create(req_id, stub)
                                        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/typeguard/__init__.py", line 1033, in wrapper
    retval = func(*args, **kwargs)
             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/resources.py", line 1572, in _create
    stub.CreateProvider(serialized)
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/grpc_client.py", line 103, in wrapper
    self.handle_grpc_error(e)
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/grpc_client.py", line 119, in handle_grpc_error
    self._handle_expected_error(e)
  File "/app/feastenv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/featureform/grpc_client.py", line 147, in _handle_expected_error
    raise Exception(detailed_message) from (e if self.debug else None)
Exception: Internal Error: cannot create iterator from type *provider_config.HDFSFileStoreConfig
```

### What mode are you running Featureform in?

Local

### What version of Python are you running?

3.10

### Featureform Python Package Version

backend: featureformcom/featureform:latest, client: featureform="1.15.8"

### Featureform Helm Chart Version

_No response_

title: ScyllaDB support
author: mkorolyov
body:
Hi there,

I’ve tested locally that the current Cassandra provider works well with ScyllaDB, which is a high-performance, drop-in replacement for Cassandra. I’d like to propose a small update to the documentation noting that ScyllaDB is also a viable option for the Inference Store.

Would you be open to accepting a PR that adds this clarification?

title: [Bug]: Invalid JSON error when specifying S3 output location in Spark Provider
author: TokuiNico
body:
### Expected Behavior

I am currently setting up a Featureform environment for my team. I have deployed the featureformcom/featureform:0.14.0 container as an all-in-one service and installed the necessary Spark-related packages, as I require Spark as the backend.

### Actual Behavior

After installing all the services and configuring the backend, I encountered the error `Invalid JSON: outputLocation:s3a://test-bucket/dev/featureform/HealthCheck/health_check_out`

Based on the error message, it seems that when Featureform executes offline_store_spark_runner.py, there is an issue with the provided input. The system expects a JSON-formatted string, but the actual input does not meet this expectation

### Steps To Reproduce

The custom featureform docker image is:
```
FROM featureformcom/featureform:0.14.0

RUN apt update && apt install -y build-essential libssl-dev zlib1g-dev \
libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev curl git \
libncursesw5-dev xz-utils tk-dev libxml2-dev libxmlsec1-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev

RUN curl https://pyenv.run | bash

ENV PYENV_ROOT /root/.pyenv
ENV PATH $PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH

RUN bash -c 'eval "$(pyenv init --path)" && \
    eval "$(pyenv init -)" && \
    pyenv install 3.8.16 && \
    pyenv global 3.8.16'

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y default-jdk

ENV JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
ENV PATH=$PATH:$JAVA_HOME/bin

RUN bash -c 'eval "$(pyenv init --path)" && \
    eval "$(pyenv init -)" && \
    pip install pyspark==3.4.0'

```

after run the container, deploy destination.py to set backend
```
import featureform as ff

s3_file_store = ff.register_s3(
    's3',
    credentials=ff.AWSAssumeRoleCredentials(),
    bucket_name='test-bucket',
    path='dev',
    bucket_region='ap-southeast-1',
)

spark_offline_store = ff.register_spark(
    'spark',
    executor=ff.SparkCredentials(
        master='spark://host.docker.internal:7077',
        deploy_mode='client',
        python_version='3.8.16',
    ),
    filestore=s3_file_store,
)
```

The Spark service is installed on the same machine, so the host is set to `host.docker.internal` to allow the Featureform container to connect to it.


If you need any additional assistance, feel free to let me know

### What mode are you running Featureform in?

Local

### What version of Python are you running?

3.9

### Featureform Python Package Version

v1.14.0

### Featureform Helm Chart Version

_No response_

### Kubernetes Version

_No response_

### Relevant log output

```shell
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/provider/scripts/spark/offline_store_spark_runner.py", line 1620, in <module>
    main(parse_args())
  File "/app/provider/scripts/spark/offline_store_spark_runner.py", line 1489, in parse_args
    arguments = parser.parse_args(args)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1768, in parse_args
    args, argv = self.parse_known_args(args, namespace)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1800, in parse_known_args
    namespace, args = self._parse_known_args(args, namespace)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1988, in _parse_known_args
    positionals_end_index = consume_positionals(start_index)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1965, in consume_positionals
    take_action(action, args)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1874, in take_action
    action(self, namespace, argument_values, option_string)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1159, in __call__
    subnamespace, arg_strings = parser.parse_known_args(arg_strings, None)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1800, in parse_known_args
    namespace, args = self._parse_known_args(args, namespace)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 2006, in _parse_known_args
    start_index = consume_optional(start_index)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1946, in consume_optional
    take_action(action, args, option_string)
  File "/root/.pyenv/versions/3.8.16/lib/python3.8/argparse.py", line 1874, in take_action
    action(self, namespace, argument_values, option_string)
  File "/app/provider/scripts/spark/offline_store_spark_runner.py", line 1600, in __call__
    raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"Invalid JSON: {values}")
argparse.ArgumentTypeError: Invalid JSON: outputLocation:s3a://test-bucket/dev/featureform/HealthCheck/health_check_out
```

title: Title: Invalid JSON error when specifying S3 output location in Spark Provider
author: TokuiNico
body:
Hi, I'm currently setting up a Featureform environment for my team.
I'm using an all-in-one image with additional packages as our testing environment.

Current Architecture:
```
- server
    - featureform service
    - spark service
- AWS
    - S3
```

Dockerfile
```
FROM featureformcom/featureform:0.14.0

RUN apt update && apt install -y build-essential libssl-dev zlib1g-dev \
libbz2-dev libreadline-dev libsqlite3-dev curl git \
libncursesw5-dev xz-utils tk-dev libxml2-dev libxmlsec1-dev libffi-dev liblzma-dev

RUN curl https://pyenv.run | bash

ENV PYENV_ROOT /root/.pyenv
ENV PATH $PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH

RUN bash -c 'eval "$(pyenv init --path)" && \
    eval "$(pyenv init -)" && \
    pyenv install 3.8.16 && \
    pyenv global 3.8.16'

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y default-jdk

ENV JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
ENV PATH=$PATH:$JAVA_HOME/bin

RUN bash -c 'eval "$(pyenv init --path)" && \
    eval "$(pyenv init -)" && \
    pip install pyspark==3.4.0'

```

definitions.py
```
import featureform as ff

s3_file_store = ff.register_s3(
    's3',
    credentials=ff.AWSAssumeRoleCredentials(),
    bucket_name='test-bucket',
    path='dev',
    bucket_region='ap-southeast-1',
)

spark_offline_store = ff.register_spark(

        master='spark://host.docker.internal:7077',
        deploy_mode='client',

)
```

I encountered an error after applying definitions.py..."

title: [Bug]: Confusing helm chart configuration
author: TokuiNico
body:
### Expected Behavior

Hi, I recently tried to install featureform using a helm chart, but I encountered quite a few problems during the installation.
Here are the commands I used for the installation:

```bash
helm upgrade --install featureform featureform/featureform \
--namespace featureform \
--create-namespace \
--set repository=featureformcom \
--set nginx.enabled=false \
--set cert.publicCert=false \
--set cert.selfSignedCert=false \
--set cert.letsencryptProd=false \
--set logging.enabled=false \
--set psql.createSecret=true \
```



### Actual Behavior

The first problem I encountered is that the chart's repository is featureformenterprise, but most of the images are in the featureformcom repo.
When I set `--set repository=featureformcom`, most of the pods can be successfully created, except for search-loader; this pod is still in the featureformenterprise repo.
The second issue is that PostgreSQL seems to be necessary for the featureform service, but this isn't specifically mentioned in the documentation.

### Steps To Reproduce

```bash
helm upgrade --install featureform featureform/featureform \
--namespace featureform \
--create-namespace \
--set repository=featureformcom \
--set nginx.enabled=false \
--set cert.publicCert=false \
--set cert.selfSignedCert=false \
--set cert.letsencryptProd=false \
--set logging.enabled=false \
--set psql.createSecret=true \
```

### What mode are you running Featureform in?

Hosted

### What version of Python are you running?

3.12

### Featureform Python Package Version

v1.14.0

### Featureform Helm Chart Version

0.14

### Kubernetes Version

1.32

### Relevant log output

```shell

```

title: Make gRPC message size configurable in streamer_proxy
author: coderabbitai[bot]
body:
Currently, the gRPC message size is hardcoded to 20MB in streamer_proxy/main.go. This should be made configurable through environment variables to allow for different message size requirements in different deployments.

Backlinks:
- PR: https://github.com/featureform/featureform/pull/1551
- Comment: https://github.com/featureform/featureform/pull/1551#discussion_r1915433125

title: [Feature Request]: make provider mod golang redis client  backward  compatibility  low version redis server
author: realfirst
body:
### Description

Our ML data infrastructure relies on historical Redis servers running versions below 5.0, which use the RESP2 protocol. However, the current code base in the `ff` repository defaults to using the RESP3 protocol for Redis communication.

To address this, I modified `provider/redis.go` to include the following option:

```go
rueidis.ClientOption{
    AlwaysRESP2: true,
}
```

After making this change, I rebuilt the Docker image for `api-server` from the `main` branch. However, the new Docker image fails to work with the `featureformcom/metadata:v0.12.5` image, which was deployed using the latest Helm chart.

I suspect the issue stems from a version mismatch. The Helm app version is `v0.12.5`, but the latest available release of `ff` in the Git repository is `0.12.2`. This discrepancy is causing confusion, and I'm seeking guidance on how to align the versions between the Git repo and the Helm chart deployment.



### Current Behavior

Connection
                                                                                            Error:
                                                                                            Featureform
                                                                                            could not
                                                                                            connect to
                                                                                            the
                                                                                            provider
                                                                                            during
                                                                                            health
                                                                                            check:
                                                                                            [PROXY]
                                                                                            unsupported
                                                                                            command
                                                                                            HELLO
                                                                                            addr:
                                                                                            r2m-auth-p…
                                                                                            provider:
                                                                                            REDIS_ONLI…
                                                                                            action:
                                                                                            client
                                                                                            initializa…”

### Desired Behavior

wish golang redis client add option to user to make choose of resp2/resp3.

### Benefits

with this option being added, existing features which already stroed in lower version redis server can be register to ff.

### Possible Implementation

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [Bug]: grpc error when applying definitions.py in local development environment
author: NikhilKr872
body:
### Expected Behavior

ff should register all providers, sources and transformations

### Actual Behavior

Getting Error 
**grpc._channel._InactiveRpcError: <_InactiveRpcError of RPC that terminated with:
        status = StatusCode.INTERNAL
        details = "grpc: error unmarshalling request: string field contains invalid UTF-8"
        debug_error_string = "UNKNOWN:Error received from peer  {created_time:"2024-09-13T11:40:34.503399+05:30", grpc_status:13, grpc_message:"grpc: error unmarshalling request: string field contains invalid UTF-8"}"**

### Steps To Reproduce

python -m venv .venv && . .venv/bin/activate
./gen_grpc.sh
./pip_update.sh
featureform deploy docker --quickstart
featureform apply quickstart/definitions.py --insecure

### What mode are you running Featureform in?

Local

3.9

1.12.6

title: [Feature Request]: Chronological Data Split
author: fumoboy007
body:
### Description

I need to split my dataset into training and test sets chronologically rather than randomly.

In order to do so, I either need to be able to specify the sort order when retrieving the dataset via `client.training_set` or to have `client.training_set` return a timestamp column. The latter used to be possible with the `include_label_timestamp` parameter but the parameter was removed in #1434.

### Current Behavior

`client.training_set` order is unspecified.

### Desired Behavior

- `client.training_set` is sorted by timestamp and documented; or
- `client.training_set` returns a timestamp column

### Benefits

Enables the chronological data split use case.

### Possible Implementation

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [Bug]: Quickstart deploy - ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'google.rpc'
author: danbaron63
body:
### Expected Behavior

The following command should execute successfully:
```
featureform deploy docker --quickstart
```

### Actual Behavior

Command fails with the following error:
```
$ featureform deploy docker --quickstart

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/xxx/code/featureform-demo/.venv/bin/featureform", line 5, in <module>
    from featureform.cli import cli
  File "/Users/xxx/code/featureform-demo/.venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/featureform/__init__.py", line 19, in <module>
    from .register import *
  File "/Users/xxx/code/featureform-demo/.venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/featureform/register.py", line 21, in <module>
    from .grpc_client import GrpcClient
  File "/Users/xxx/code/featureform-demo/.venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/featureform/grpc_client.py", line 8, in <module>
    from google.rpc import error_details_pb2, status_pb2
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'google.rpc'
```

### Steps To Reproduce

```
pyenv local 3.10
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip3 install -q --upgrade pip
.venv/bin/pip3 install featureform
featureform deploy docker --quickstart
```

### What mode are you running Featureform in?

Local

### What version of Python are you running?

3.10

### Featureform Python Package Version

1.12.6

### Featureform Helm Chart Version

_No response_

### Kubernetes Version

_No response_

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### Additional Information
Platform: MacOS Sonoma, Version 14.5, Apple M1 Max.

page status with no error on Dashboard

### Actual Behavior

out of a box helm deployment

```shell
In etcd pod logs not see directly issues that cause the 500 error

The featureform-feature-server is starting without any errors. It doesn’t seem to be related to the issue.

featureform-metadata-server log indicates it failed to read an MD5 hash file and is using a fallback value instead, not sure if this might or not directly cause the 500 error?
```

title: [Bug]: Error when applying `definitions.py`
author: DeCockPieter-Jan
body:
### Expected Behavior

When applying the `definitions.py` file, Featureform should successfully register all providers, sources, transformations, features...

ClickHouse and Redis are used as providers.

### Actual Behavior

When applying the `definitions.py` file, Featureform successfully register the providers, sources and transformations, but returns an error when trying to register a feature and label: `transformation not found` and `table not found`.

```
COMPLETED                                                                                                                                                                                                
 Resource Type              Name (Variant)                                      Status      Error                                                                                                        
 Provider                   clickhouse ()                                       READY                                                                                                                    
 Provider                   redis ()                                            READY                                                                                                                    
 SourceVariant              metric_events (2024-05-09t20-40-15)                 READY                                                                                                                    
 Transformation             hourly_metric_average (2024-05-09t20-40-15)         READY                                                                                                                    
 FeatureVariant             hourly_avg (2024-05-09t20-40-15)                    FAILED       transformation not found: featureform_primary__hourly_metric_average__2024-05-09t20-40-15                   
 LabelVariant               value_bool (2024-05-09t20-40-15)                    FAILED       Table metric_events Variant 2024-05-09t20-40-15 not found.                                                  
 TrainingSetVariant         metrics_training (2024-05-09t20-40-15)              FAILED       feature could not complete job: feature registration failed: name: hourly_avg, variant: 2024-05-09t20-40-15
```

### Steps To Reproduce

- Deploy Featureform on (local) K8s cluster using Helm
- Portforward Featureform API server:`kubectl port-forward --namespace featureform svc/featureform-api-server 7878:7878 &`
- Set Featureform host env variable: `export FEATUREFORM_HOST=localhost:7878`
- Define providers, sources, transformations, features, labels... in `definitions.py`
- Apply definitions: `featureform apply definitions.py --insecure`

### What mode are you running Featureform in?

Local

### What version of Python are you running?

3.10

### Featureform Python Package Version

1.12.6

### Featureform Helm Chart Version

0.12.1

### Kubernetes Version

1.27.3

### Relevant log output

_No response_
